summary: Triage a CI failure, isolate the likely cause, and recommend the smallest corrective action with rerun guidance.

inputs.name: failure_log
inputs.description: Job logs, failing step, and environment details.

inputs.name: pipeline_config
inputs.description: Current CI workflow or pipeline definition.

inputs.name: recent_change_context
inputs.description: Recent code, dependency, or infrastructure changes around the failure.

outputs.name: root_cause_hypothesis
outputs.description: Most likely failure class with supporting evidence.

outputs.name: fix_plan
outputs.description: Minimal remediation steps and owner guidance.

outputs.name: rerun_checklist
outputs.description: Validation steps before rerunning the pipeline.

budget.notes: Favor evidence-backed root cause isolation over broad rebuilds.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is CI failure diagnosis, not generic app debugging or release approval.
Require the failing step or log excerpt before proposing infrastructure changes.
Avoid secret exposure and pin any recommended workflow changes to explicit versions.
Return a minimal fix plan and rerun checklist with rollback notes.

steps.action: Identify the failing stage, likely failure class, and reproducibility hints.
Determine blast radius, urgency, and whether the failure blocks releases or critical checks.
Check environment, dependency, cache, and infrastructure assumptions implicated by the logs.
Merge the findings into one root-cause hypothesis, fix plan, and rerun checklist.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read workflow configs, logs, and supporting repo files.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Write a triage note or patch file when requested.

verification.method: Check the analysis points to a concrete failing step or log section.
Check the plan avoids unrelated pipeline rewrites.
Check the output includes validation and rerun guidance.

success_criteria: The triage identifies a plausible root cause with evidence from the failing run.
The fix plan is scoped to the failure and includes a safe rerun path.
Secret handling and action-version hygiene are preserved in any recommended change.

rollback.when: If the proposed fix broadens scope without evidence, revert to the last known failing step and regenerate only the runtime and incident analysis.
